visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you are married to, or are the registered partner or cohabitant of a person who is living in Norway and be EU/EEA citizen, you are entitled to a family immigration permit if you otherwise
                                                                        meet the criteria.

## Visa Fees
In order to know more details about tariff of all kind of long term visas/ residence permits, please visit website of The Directorate of Immigration (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/).
Note that the visa fees must be paid online using a credit or debit card when you fill in the form. 
- VFS Service Fee (effective from 01 September 2025) is EUR 25 (VND 775,000).

The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay EUR 22,5 (VND 698,000) to cover the Resubmission Courier.
- Service Fee and Additional Service Fees can be paid by cash, debit card or credit card
- The following are exempted from fee for submitting application for Schengen visa:
- Children under 6 years of age
- Pupils, students and their accompanying teachers if the purpose of the travel is studying or other training
- Researchers from third countries travelling in Norway and the Schengen-area to do scientific research as defined by the European parliament and council recommendation.
- Family members of EU and Swiss citizens.

## Documents Required
Please have a look at this link (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/):
Important Noted: All documents must be in A4 paper and NO staples, pins

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Standards:
All visa applications must include one recent photograph in color meeting the compulsory internationally agreed standards.
The basic rules are simple:
- The photo must not be older than 6 months
- Taken against a plain light background
- Size 35mm by 45mm (IMPORTANT: Passport photos in U.S. format are not accepted)
- Face must cover 2/3 of photo and looking straight forward
- Neutral expression please and mouth must be closed (no obvious grinning or raised eyebrows)
- Eyes must be on the middle line or in upper half of the photograph
- Hair must not cover face
- No reflection or glare on your glasses and the frames should not cover your eyes (you may find it easier to remove your glasses)
- The photograph must be in sharp focus and clear
- Have a strong definition between the face and background
- Printed professionally
- Eyes open and clearly visible
- Full head, without any head covering unless it is worn for religious beliefs or medical reasons
- CHILDREN: photographs of children must show a clear image that is a true likeness of the child. The eyes should be open and if you need to support a baby’s head, it is important,
                                                                                    that your hand cannot be seen in the photograph.

## Processing Time
For residence permit application, the Embassy will forward your application to the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration for considering. Information about case-processing times is intended
                                                                        as a guide. Some cases make take shorter or longer time to process. For details, please check here (http://www.udi.no/Norwegian-Directorate-of-Immigration/Oversiktsider/Case-processing-times/).

## Download Forms
This need to be completed by the applicant online, please kindly refer to this link: https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/application-portal/> (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/application-portal/).

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Foreign spouses of Norwegian citizens are, as a general rule, required to submit their applications for a residence permit at the Norwegian Embassy, and are not permitted to enter Norway until a residence permit has been granted by the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI).
However, in some exceptional cases, the Embassy may grant a 7-day Entry Visa (a national D-visa) so that the foreign spouse can travel to Norway to submit the application for Residence Permit, and wait in Norway while the case is being processed. Similarly, a 7-day Entry Visa may be granted if an application for Residence Permit already has been submitted. The Embassy processes the case, but a rejection may be appealed to UDI Applications for 7-day entry visas (D-visas) are to be registered on the Application Portal.
Simply fill out the same application form as for a Schengen Visa, pay the handling fee of 80 Euros using a credit/ debit card, and book an appointment for when you would like to submit your passport and supporting documents at the Norwegian Visa Application Centre. The processing time for 7-day Entry Visas is eight (16) weeks.
The insurance policy must be valid for the entire Schengen Area, for the entire duration of your stay, and it must provide minimum cover of €30,000.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Foreign spouses of Norwegian citizens are, as a general rule, required to submit their applications for a residence permit at the Norwegian Embassy, and are not permitted to enter Norway until a residence permit has been granted by the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI).
However, in some exceptional cases, the Embassy may grant a 7-day Entry Visa (a national D-visa) so that the foreign spouse can travel to Norway to submit the application for Residence Permit, and wait in Norway while the case is being processed. Similarly, a 7-day Entry Visa may be granted if an application for Residence Permit already has been submitted. The Embassy processes the case, but a rejection may be appealed to UDI Applications for 7-day entry visas (D-visas) are to be registered on the Application Portal.
Simply fill out the same application form as for a Schengen Visa, pay the handling fee of 80 Euros using a credit/ debit card, and book an appointment for when you would like to submit your passport and supporting documents at the Norwegian Visa Application Centre. The processing time for 7-day Entry Visas is eight (16) weeks.
The insurance policy must be valid for the entire Schengen Area, for the entire duration of your stay, and it must provide minimum cover of €30,000.

## Visa Fees
Please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3598) to check visa fee
Please Note:
Applicants who register their applications online using the Application Portal must pay the handling/processing fee of 80 Euros with a credit/debit card (Mastercard or Visa). Please be informed that all the fees are non-refundable.
There is a service charge levied per application over and above the visa handling fee.
Note that the visa fees must be paid online using a credit or debit card when you fill in the form. 
- VFS Service Fee (effective from 01 September 2025) is EUR 25 (VND 775,000).
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay EUR 22,5 (VND 698,000) to cover the Resubmission Courier.
- Service Fee and Additional Service Fees can be paid by cash, debit card or credit card
- The following are exempted from fee for submitting application for Schengen visa:
- Children under 6 years of age
- Pupils, students and their accompanying teachers if the purpose of the travel is studying or other training
- Researchers from third countries travelling in Norway and the Schengen-area to do scientific research as defined by the European parliament and council recommendation.
- Family members of EU and Swiss citizens.

## Documents Required
Please refer this link (https://www.udi.no/en/have-applied/family-immigration/#link-1228) for preparing document required

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Standards:

- Face must cover 2/3 of photo and looking straight forward
- Neutral expression please and mouth must be closed (no obvious grinning or raised eyebrows)
- Eyes must be on the middle line or in upper half of the photograph
- Hair must not cover face
- No reflection or glare on your glasses and the frames should not cover your eyes (you may find it easier to remove your glasses)
- The photograph must be in sharp focus and clear
- Have a strong definition between the face and background
- Printed professionally
- Eyes open and clearly visible
- Full head, without any head covering unless it is worn for religious beliefs or medical reasons
- CHILDREN: photographs of children must show a clear image that is a true likeness of the child. The eyes should be open and if you need to support a baby’s head, it is important,

visa_types.name: Resident Permits
visa_types.overview:
For Norway, all permits that require a stay for longer than 90 days are called residence permits. The grounds for applying a residence permit can be work, family reunion, studies etc.
Please note that if you plan to work in Norway you will, as a main rule, need a residence permit even if the stay is planned to be shorter than 90 days.
To read about the different residence permits and requirements please see the webpages of the Directorate of Immigration in Norway (UDI). By entering general information about your nationality and
                                                            the reason for applying you will get speciffic information on what documents to present, case processing times etc.

## Overview
This visa is solely for the purpose of undertaking journeys by members of official delegations who, following an official invitation, participate in meetings, consultations, negotiations or exchange programs, as well as events held in Norway by intergovernmental organizations.
The insurance policy must be valid for the entire Schengen Area, for the entire duration of your stay, and it must provide minimum cover of €30,000.
Important Information of Group Applications:
Group applications must indicate the same period of travel and itinerary. Visa applications for all applicants listed in group registration, must be submitted at VFS on the same date.
If one or more applicant are missing to show up in person at VFS, the following documents are required to submit instead:
A letter from the applicant stating that they wish to cancel their online visa application (which was registered as part of the group).
A request letter for a visa fee refunded.
The applicant who is missing to submit their application together with the group should provide the above two letters, together with the group. A new online application can be registered and be submitted as an individual visa application later.

## Visa Fees

[Table:Business]
Note that the visa fees must be paid online using a credit or debit card when you fill in the form. 
- VFS Service Fee (effective from 01 September 2025) is EUR 25 (VND 775,000).
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay EUR 22,5 (VND 698,000) to cover the Resubmission Courier.
- Service Fee and Additional Service Fees can be paid by cash, debit card or credit card
- The following are exempted from fee for submitting application for Schengen visa:
- Children under 6 years of age
- Pupils, students and their accompanying teachers if the purpose of the travel is studying or other training
- Researchers from third countries travelling in Norway and the Schengen-area to do scientific research as defined by the European parliament and council recommendation.
- Family members of EU and Swiss citizens.

## Documents Required
Checklist for Official Visit 
Power of Attorney Form 
Important Note:
- Important Noted: All documents must be in A4 paper and NO staples, pins. Applicants are required to bring original documents for checking at VFS on submission date.
- For more information, please click here (http://www.norway.org.vn/studywork/visaandresidence/Schengen-visa-C/) 

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Standards:
All visa applications must include one recent photograph in color meeting the compulsory internationally agreed standards.
The basic rules are simple:
- The photo must not be older than 6 months
- Taken against a plain light background
- Size 35mm by 45mm (IMPORTANT: Passport photos in U.S. format are not accepted)
- Face must cover 2/3 of photo and looking straight forward
- Neutral expression please and mouth must be closed (no obvious grinning or raised eyebrows)
- Eyes must be on the middle line or in upper half of the photograph
- Hair must not cover face
- No reflection or glare on your glasses and the frames should not cover your eyes (you may find it easier to remove your glasses)
- The photograph must be in sharp focus and clear
- Have a strong definition between the face and background
- Printed professionally
- Eyes open and clearly visible
- Full head, without any head covering unless it is worn for religious beliefs or medical reasons
- CHILDREN: photographs of children must show a clear image that is a true likeness of the child. The eyes should be open and if you need to support a baby’s head, it is important,
                                                                                    that your hand cannot be seen in the photograph.

## Processing Time
The processing time for applications is normally up to 15 calendar days after the embassy or consulate has received the application. In some cases, applications are sent to the Directorate of Immigration (UDI), and the processing time in these cases may be up to 45 calendar days.
Please refer this link ( https://www.norway.no/en/thailand/services-info/visitors-visa-res-permit/visitors-visa/#processing-time)

It is possible to get a visa in connection with a business visit in Norway

                                                                        in your country of origin.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
The purpose of the au pair scheme is cultural exchange. As au pairs, young people can improve their language skills and knowledge of Norway and Norwegian society by living with a Norwegian
                                                                        family. In return, the au pair performs services such as light housework and child care for the host family. You can be granted a residence permit as an au pair for a maximum period
                                                                        of two years. If the offer of employment is for less than two years, you can only be granted a residence permit for the period covered by the employment offer.
For more information about au pair permits, please click here (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/au-pair/)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The purpose of the au pair scheme is cultural exchange. As au pairs, young people can improve their language skills and knowledge of Norway and Norwegian society by living with a Norwegian
                                                                        family. In return, the au pair performs services such as light housework and child care for the host family. You can be granted a residence permit as an au pair for a maximum period
                                                                        of two years. If the offer of employment is for less than two years, you can only be granted a residence permit for the period covered by the employment offer.

- Family members of EU and Swiss citizens.

Please have a look at this link (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/):

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Standards:
All visa applications must include one recent photograph in color meeting the compulsory internationally agreed standards.
The basic rules are simple:
- The photo must not be older than 6 months
- Taken against a plain light background
- Size 35mm by 45mm (IMPORTANT: Passport photos in U.S. format are not accepted)
- Face must cover 2/3 of photo and looking straight forward
- Neutral expression please and mouth must be closed (no obvious grinning or raised eyebrows)
- Eyes must be on the middle line or in upper half of the photograph
- Hair must not cover face
- No reflection or glare on your glasses and the frames should not cover your eyes (you may find it easier to remove your glasses)
- The photograph must be in sharp focus and clear
- Have a strong definition between the face and background
- Printed professionally
- Eyes open and clearly visible
- Full head, without any head covering unless it is worn for religious beliefs or medical reasons

                                                                                    that your hand cannot be seen in the photograph.

## Processing Time
For residence permit application, the Embassy will forward your application to the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration for considering. Information about case-processing times is intended
                                                                        as a guide. Some cases make take shorter or longer time to process. For details, please check here (http://www.udi.no/Norwegian-Directorate-of-Immigration/Oversiktsider/Case-processing-times/).

visa_types.overview:
Overview
The following information applies to those who are seafarer and need the short-stay visa to boarding from Norway port.
You may submit your application up to 09 months before your planned departure.
You will need to submit a copy of a medical travel insurance policy that will cover any costs of repatriation on medical grounds, urgent medical care, or emergency hospital treatment.
The insurance policy must be valid for the entire Schengen Area, for the entire duration of your stay, and it must provide minimum cover of €30,000.
Important Information of Group Applications:
Group applications must indicate the same period of travel and itinerary. Visa applications for all applicants listed in group registration, must be submitted at VFS on the same date.
If one or more applicant are missing to show up in person at VFS, the following documents are required to submit instead:
A letter from the applicant stating that they wish to cancel their online visa application (which was registered as part of the group).
A request letter for a visa fee refunded.
The applicant who is missing to submit their application together with the group should provide the above two letters, together with the group. A new online application can be registered and be submitted as an individual visa application later.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa is solely for the purpose of leisure travel including vacationing, cruising, sightseeing and other recreational activities.
You will need to submit a copy of a medical travel insurance policy that will cover any costs of repatriation on medical grounds, urgent medical care, or emergency hospital treatment.
The insurance policy must be valid for the entire Schengen Area, for the entire duration of your stay, and it must provide minimum cover of €30,000.
Important Information of Group Applications:
Group applications must indicate the same period of travel and itinerary. Visa applications for all applicants listed in group registration, must be submitted at VFS on the same date.
If one or more applicant are missing to show up in person at VFS, the following documents are required to submit instead:
A letter from the applicant stating that they wish to cancel their online visa application (which was registered as part of the group).
A request letter for a visa fee refunded.
The applicant who is missing to submit their application together with the group should provide the above two letters, together with the group. A new online application can be registered and be submitted as an individual visa application later.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This visa is solely for the purpose of leisure travel including vacationing, cruising, sightseeing and other recreational activities.
You will need to submit a copy of a medical travel insurance policy that will cover any costs of repatriation on medical grounds, urgent medical care, or emergency hospital treatment.
The insurance policy must be valid for the entire Schengen Area, for the entire duration of your stay, and it must provide minimum cover of €30,000.
Important Information of Group Applications:
Group applications must indicate the same period of travel and itinerary. Visa applications for all applicants listed in group registration, must be submitted at VFS on the same date.
If one or more applicant are missing to show up in person at VFS, the following documents are required to submit instead:
A letter from the applicant stating that they wish to cancel their online visa application (which was registered as part of the group).
A request letter for a visa fee refunded.
The applicant who is missing to submit their application together with the group should provide the above two letters, together with the group. A new online application can be registered and be submitted as an individual visa application later.

## Visa Fees

[Table:Business]
Note that the visa fees must be paid online using a credit or debit card when you fill in the form. 
- VFS Service Fee (effective from 01 September 2025) is EUR 25 (VND 775,000).
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay EUR 22,5 (VND 698,000) to cover the Resubmission Courier.
- Service Fee and Additional Service Fees can be paid by cash, debit card or credit card
- The following are exempted from fee for submitting application for Schengen visa:
- Children under 6 years of age
- Pupils, students and their accompanying teachers if the purpose of the travel is studying or other training
- Researchers from third countries travelling in Norway and the Schengen-area to do scientific research as defined by the European parliament and council recommendation.
- Family members of EU and Swiss citizens.

## Documents Required
Checklist for Tourist Visa to Norway
Power of Attorney Form 
Important Note:
- All documents must be in A4 paper size
- NO staples, pins.
- All documents in Vietnamese language mark (*) on checklist need to be notarized copy at local authority first and be translated to English language.
- The documents in Vietnamese language without mark (*) on checklist are strongly encouraged to be translated into English or Norwegian.
For more information, please click here (https://www.norway.no/en/vietnam)

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Standards:
All visa applications must include one recent photograph in color meeting the compulsory internationally agreed standards.
The basic rules are simple:
- The photo must not be older than 6 months
- Taken against a plain light background
- Size 35mm by 45mm (IMPORTANT: Passport photos in U.S. format are not accepted)
- Face must cover 2/3 of photo and looking straight forward
- Neutral expression please and mouth must be closed (no obvious grinning or raised eyebrows)
- Eyes must be on the middle line or in upper half of the photograph
- Hair must not cover face
- No reflection or glare on your glasses and the frames should not cover your eyes (you may find it easier to remove your glasses)
- The photograph must be in sharp focus and clear
- Have a strong definition between the face and background
- Printed professionally
- Eyes open and clearly visible
- Full head, without any head covering unless it is worn for religious beliefs or medical reasons
- CHILDREN: photographs of children must show a clear image that is a true likeness of the child. The eyes should be open and if you need to support a baby’s head, it is important,
                                                                                    that your hand cannot be seen in the photograph.

## Processing Time
The processing time for applications is normally up to 15 calendar days after the embassy or consulate has received the application. In some cases, applications are sent to the Directorate of Immigration (UDI), and the processing time in these cases may be up to 45 calendar days.
Please refer this link ( https://www.norway.no/en/thailand/services-info/visitors-visa-res-permit/visitors-visa/#processing-time)

visa_types.name: Schengen Visa Type (C)
visa_types.overview:
Norway is a part of the Schengen co-operation. Nationals of countries participating in this cooperation may travel freely between the member countries. The same applies to foreign nationals holding a residence permit in one of the member countries. The 29 member states are Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Croatia, Czechia, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, the Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden and Switzerland.
The Norwegian Embassy in Bangkok, Thailand issues Schengen visas to citizens and residents of Vietnam and Lao PDR for travel to Norway. The Schengen visa granted for Norway is valid in all countries in Europe that are part of the Schengen Agreement. Please be aware that some third country citizens are sent for extra consultations, the Schengen visa processing time will in these cases therefore be longer. Schengen Visas are short-term visas for up to 90 days in the Schengen area in the course of the last six months. After a period of 90 days in Schengen, the applicant is not allowed to enter Schengen again before a new 90 day period has passed. Applicants who intend to work or stay more than 90 days must apply for a residence permit.
